open_questions: Confirm: Self-improvement runs use the same Reviewer agent config (simpleComplexityAgent/complexComplexityAgent) as per-task review. No separate agent config for self-improvement in v1?
Confirm: Weekly schedule = once per week at a fixed time (e.g. Sunday 00:00 UTC). No user-configurable day/time for weekly in v1?

Confirm: Improvement tasks are created with no parent (top-level) and no automatic link to the plan that triggered the run, unless we add optional planId/runId in task extra. Proceed with optional planId in extra for traceability?

tasks.title: Add self-improvement types and settings fields to ProjectSettings
tasks.description: Add to shared ProjectSettings: selfImprovementFrequency?: 'never' | 'after_each_plan' | 'daily' | 'weekly' (default 'never'); selfImprovementLastRunAt?: string (ISO 8601); selfImprovementLastCommitSha?: string. Add VALID_SELF_IMPROVEMENT_FREQUENCIES and SELF_IMPROVEMENT_FREQUENCY_OPTIONS for UI. In parseSettings(), parse and validate selfImprovementFrequency; default to 'never' for existing projects. Do not allow client to set lastRunAt/lastCommitSha via parseSettings — backend will set these only after a run. Acceptance: existing tests pass; new unit test in packages/shared/src/__tests__/settings.test.ts asserts valid frequencies and default. Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern=settings.test

tasks.title: Implement git-based change detection for self-improvement
tasks.description: Create a small utility (e.g. in packages/backend/src/services/self-improvement-change-detection.ts or utils): given repoPath, optional sinceIso (selfImprovementLastRunAt), optional sinceCommitSha (selfImprovementLastCommitSha), and optional baseBranch (from settings.worktreeBaseBranch), return whether the codebase has changed (e.g. any commits on base branch since that time/SHA). Use git log --since or compare HEAD to sinceCommitSha. When sinceIso/sinceCommitSha are both missing, treat as 'changed' (first run). Handle empty repo / no git: return false or document and treat as unchanged to avoid unnecessary runs. Export a single async function hasRepoChangedSince(repoPath, options): Promise<boolean>. Acceptance: unit tests with mocked git or temp repos assert changed/unchanged for various histories. Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern=self-improvement-change-detection

tasks.title: Expose self-improvement settings in GET/PUT project settings API
tasks.description: Ensure GET /projects/:id/settings response includes selfImprovementFrequency, selfImprovementLastRunAt, selfImprovementLastCommitSha (from ProjectSettings). PUT /projects/:id/settings must accept selfImprovementFrequency; validate to one of the four values. Backend must NOT allow client to set selfImprovementLastRunAt or selfImprovementLastCommitSha — ignore or strip these from request body in project.service.updateSettings so only internal runs update them. Files: packages/backend/src/services/project.service.ts (parse/validate frequency, strip lastRun/lastSha on put), packages/backend/src/routes/projects.ts (no change if response is full settings object). Add integration test in projects-route or settings-lifecycle test that PUT with selfImprovementFrequency persists and GET returns it; PUT with lastRunAt is ignored. Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern=projects-route|settings-lifecycle
dependsOn: Add self-improvement types and settings fields to ProjectSettings

tasks.title: Create self-improvement service: run review and create tasks
tasks.description: Add SelfImprovementService (e.g. packages/backend/src/services/self-improvement.service.ts) that: (1) checks only one run per project at a time (in-memory or small lock per projectId). (2) Runs change detection (hasRepoChangedSince) for repoPath and project settings lastRun/lastSha; if unchanged, return without running. (3) Builds codebase context (or diff since last run) and SPEC.md; uses project reviewAngles (or general review if empty). (4) Invokes Reviewer (reuse existing spawn path, e.g. AgentService.invokeReviewAgent or equivalent) with a dedicated self-improvement prompt that instructs the agent to output a structured list of improvement items (markdown list or JSON block). (5) Parses agent output into array of { title, description? }; on parse failure create one fallback task 'Self-improvement review failed to parse — please review agent output'. (6) For each item calls TaskStoreService.create(projectId, title, { description, extra: { source: 'self-improvement', planId?, runId? } }). (7) Updates project settings selfImprovementLastRunAt (and optionally selfImprovementLastCommitSha) via settings store. (8) On Reviewer failure/timeout: do not update lastRunAt; log and optionally surface in status. Use existing ContextAssembler/PlanService.getCodebaseContext where applicable. Acceptance: unit tests for parse output and task creation with correct source; integration test with mocked Reviewer. Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern=self-improvement.service
dependsOn: Add self-improvement types and settings fields to ProjectSettings
Implement git-based change detection for self-improvement

tasks.title: Trigger self-improvement after plan execution completes (after each plan)
tasks.description: When frequency is 'after_each_plan', trigger self-improvement once when plan execution is fully complete: all tasks for the plan are done and the plan work has been merged to the base branch. Hook point: in MergeCoordinatorService, immediately after the epic is closed (e.g. after runFinalReviewAndCloseOrCreateTasks closes the epic, or after the path that closes epic without final review). For the epic that was just closed, get planId from plan store (epic description or planGetByEpicId). Call SelfImprovementService.runIfDue(projectId, { trigger: 'after_each_plan', planId }). runIfDue checks settings.selfImprovementFrequency === 'after_each_plan', then change detection, then runs the self-improvement flow. Do NOT trigger when user clicks Execute; only when the last task of that plan has been merged and epic closed. Files: packages/backend/src/services/merge-coordinator.service.ts, packages/backend/src/services/self-improvement.service.ts. Acceptance: integration test that closing last task and merging (mock) does not trigger if frequency is never; when frequency is after_each_plan and changes exist, SelfImprovementService.runIfDue is invoked. Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern=merge-coordinator|self-improvement
dependsOn: Create self-improvement service: run review and create tasks

tasks.title: Add daily and weekly self-improvement scheduler
tasks.description: Create a scheduler (similar to nightly-deploy-scheduler) that runs on a fixed interval (e.g. every minute). On each tick, for each project with selfImprovementFrequency 'daily' or 'weekly': daily = run at most once per calendar day (e.g. midnight UTC); weekly = run at most once per week (e.g. Sunday 00:00 UTC). For each project due, call SelfImprovementService.runIfDue(projectId, { trigger: 'scheduled' }). Use lastRunByProject or selfImprovementLastRunAt to avoid duplicate runs. Start scheduler on backend init (index.ts) alongside nightly deploy scheduler. Files: packages/backend/src/services/self-improvement-scheduler.service.ts, packages/backend/src/index.ts. Add unit tests with fake time. Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern=self-improvement-scheduler
dependsOn: Create self-improvement service: run review and create tasks

tasks.title: Include self-improvement run status in execute status API
tasks.description: When a self-improvement run is in progress for a project, include it in GET /projects/:id/execute/status response so the UI can show a busy indicator. Add to OrchestratorStatus (packages/shared/src/types/agent.ts): selfImprovementRunInProgress?: boolean (or activeSelfImprovementRunId?: string). OrchestratorService (or a shared state that execute route reads) must set this true while SelfImprovementService is running for that project and false when done. Option: SelfImprovementService registers in a small in-memory Set<projectId> when starting and removes when finished; execute route or orchestrator builds status from that. Files: packages/shared/src/types/agent.ts, packages/shared/src/types/websocket.ts (ExecuteStatusEvent), packages/backend/src/services/orchestrator.service.ts or execute route, packages/backend/src/services/self-improvement.service.ts. Acceptance: test that status returns selfImprovementRunInProgress true only while run is active. Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern=execute|orchestrator
dependsOn: Create self-improvement service: run review and create tasks

tasks.title: Add Self-improvement frequency dropdown to Project Settings UI
tasks.description: In Project Settings, add a Self-improvement section (grouped with Code review / Review agents). Single dropdown: 'Self-improvement frequency' with options Never, After each Plan, Daily, Weekly (default Never). Use SELF_IMPROVEMENT_FREQUENCY_OPTIONS from shared. Help text: 'When the codebase has changed since the last run, a review runs using your code review lenses and creates improvement tasks.' For 'After each Plan' add: 'Runs once after a plan's execution is fully complete (all tasks done and merged), not when you click Execute.' Persist via existing PUT /projects/:id/settings (persistSettings with selfImprovementFrequency). Optional read-only: display 'Last run: <date>' when backend exposes selfImprovementLastRunAt. Files: packages/frontend/src/components/ProjectSettingsModal.tsx, packages/shared (already added in earlier task). Acceptance: dropdown saves and loads; help text visible. Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern=ProjectSettingsModal
dependsOn: Add self-improvement types and settings fields to ProjectSettings
Expose self-improvement settings in GET/PUT project settings API

tasks.title: Show self-improvement run indicator when run is in progress
tasks.description: When execute status includes selfImprovementRunInProgress: true (or activeSelfImprovementRunId), show a non-blocking indicator in the execute/status area or near settings so users understand why new tasks might appear. Reuse execute status subscription (WebSocket or polling). Do not block the main execute UI. Files: packages/frontend (execute slice or component that shows orchestrator status — e.g. executeSlice, or component that displays 'Running' / agent list). Acceptance: when backend sets selfImprovementRunInProgress, UI shows a small indicator (e.g. 'Self-improvement review in progress'). Run: npm test and manual check.
dependsOn: Include self-improvement run status in execute status API

tasks.title: Task list: badge or filter for self-improvement tasks
tasks.description: Tasks created by self-improvement have extra.source === 'self-improvement'. Expose source (or a derived flag) in task list API response if not already (task extra is typically returned). In the frontend task list, allow users to distinguish self-improvement tasks: add a badge (e.g. 'Self-improvement') or a filter option 'Self-improvement' so users can triage. Files: packages/backend (task serialization may already include extra; ensure source is present), packages/frontend task list component and filter state. Acceptance: created improvement tasks show badge; filter by source works. Run: npm test.
dependsOn: Create self-improvement service: run review and create tasks

tasks.title: Integration test: after-each-plan trigger only on plan completion
tasks.description: Integration test that verifies: (1) When selfImprovementFrequency is 'after_each_plan' and the user clicks Execute (plan execution starts), self-improvement is NOT triggered. (2) When all tasks for the plan are closed and merged and the epic is closed (simulate or use merge-coordinator path), and change detection returns true, the self-improvement run is triggered and tasks are created with source 'self-improvement'. (3) When change detection returns false after plan completion, Reviewer is not invoked and lastRunAt is not updated. Use mocks for Reviewer and git where needed. Run: npm test -- --testPathPattern=self-improvement|merge-coordinator
dependsOn: Trigger self-improvement after plan execution completes (after each plan)
Create self-improvement service: run review and create tasks

tasks.title: Optional: GET /projects/:id/self-improvement/history endpoint
tasks.description: Deferred from v1. If implemented: add GET /projects/:id/self-improvement/history returning list of recent self-improvement runs (timestamp, status, tasksCreatedCount). Requires persisting run metadata (e.g. in project extra or a small table). Priority 4; skip if time-boxed. Run: npm test.
dependsOn: Create self-improvement service: run review and create tasks
